patches.hypothesis: The bug is caused by the Validate.notEmpty check on the 'name' parameter, which does not allow for blank or empty names. To fix this, we should replace the Validate.notEmpty with a more lenient check that allows for empty strings.
patches.changes:
- Validate.notEmpty(name);
+ if (name == null) { throw new IllegalArgumentException("String must not be null"); }
patches.diff:
- Validate.notEmpty(name);
+ if (name == null) { throw new IllegalArgumentException("String must not be null"); }

patches.hypothesis: The constructor throws an IllegalArgumentException when the 'name' parameter is empty because of the Validate.notEmpty(name) call. We need to allow for empty names to pass validation.

The bug is caused by the Validate.notEmpty(name) check which throws an exception when the name is an empty string. To fix this, we should allow the name to be empty or null without throwing an exception.
The bug is caused by the Validate.notEmpty check which throws an exception when the name is an empty string. To fix this, we should allow for empty strings and only validate non-empty strings.
The bug is caused by the Validate.notEmpty(name) call which throws an exception when the name is empty. The fix is to remove or modify this validation to allow empty names.
